mods.note: core progression backbone
tier-gating engine: items/recipes/blocks/entities/dimensions locked behind stages, FTB Teams+Quests integration for later phases
Tier 1 'dumb storage': link chests into a browsable terminal, patched to iron-tier via KubeJS (stock recipes need diamond+ender_pearl+glowstone)
Create Crafts & Additions - Alternator (kinetic->FE) and Electric Motor (FE->kinetic), unlocked at Brass Age to power Refined Storage

browsable storage network, explicitly named in instructions.md; moved to Brass Age unlock (needs Nether Quartz + diamond-tier processor, both available there), energy requirement kept on and powered by Create's own Alternator
JS engine required by KubeJS
recipe/loot scripting used for the storage tier patches here, and for the full recipe-gating pass in Phase 9
Pufferfish's Skills - RPG skill tree framework, categories authored in pack/kubejs/data/puffish_skills/
Pufferfish's Attributes - mining/pickaxe/sprinting speed, sword/ranged damage, bow/crossbow projectile speed etc used by our skill categories
required by the FTB Library/Teams/Quests chain below
FTB mod-common utility library, required by FTB Teams and FTB Quests. Not on Modrinth (FTB suite is CurseForge-exclusive) - resolved via direct CDN, see resolve_mods.py docstring
hard dependency of FTB Quests (not optional), installed in Phase 4; team_mode flipped to 'ftb_teams' in Phase 6 once real parties are wanted - see that phase's DESIGN.md notes

Tier Progression preset-track chapter only, generated by scripts/gen_quests.py into pack/config/ftbquests/quests/ (old SNBT format, not JSON5). Long-running/daily chapters moved to KubeJS in Phase 6 - see gen_achievements.py/gen_dailies.py and DESIGN.md

Create: Numismatics - coin currency (Spur/Bevel/Sprocket/Cog/Crown/Sun, physical items) + Vendor blocks for sell-to-vendor. Tier-based sell pricing generated by scripts/gen_economy.py from pack/config/ProgressiveStages/*.toml's item lists

global board for player-run Numismatics shops - browse/compare listings and navigate to a seller's persistent Vendor block from anywhere; purchase itself still happens at that block (doesn't require the seller online), not an instant remote transaction

chunk claims. All 3 required deps (Architectury API, FTB Library, FTB Teams) already installed since Phase 4 - reuses the same team/party system as FTB Quests' preset-track sharing rather than a second, parallel party concept
required by Silent Gear below
blacksmithing: modular tool/weapon assembly (rod+head+binding at a workstation) replaces flat crafting-table recipes, per instructions.md
combat variety: new weapon types with distinct movesets/playstyles, stamina, and a skill system - all-in-one rather than a separate weapon-variety + separate skill mod
required by Ars Nouveau - not listed in Modrinth's own dependency metadata for our version, found only via the actual boot error (ModLoadingException naming it directly)
required by Ars Nouveau - same as geckolib, found via the actual boot error rather than Modrinth's dependency metadata
magic system: mage archetype via spell crafting, summoner archetype via summon rituals (Summon Guardians/Wilden) + combat familiars
required by Apotheosis chain below
required by Apotheosis chain below (its in-game guidebook, technically optional per the mod but required by the jar's own dependency metadata)
required by Apotheosis
required by Apotheosis
required by Apotheosis

mob scaling: Apothic Invaders (visual light-beam+sound spawn indicator, scaled loot) and Apothic Elites cover the 'tell a stronger mob by looking at it' + 'rewards scale with difficulty' asks; affix/gem loot system covers unique boss-tier drops
required by YUNG's Better Dungeons below
overhauls vanilla's dungeon structure into larger multi-room dungeons - the 'some sort of dungeons' ask
structure variety/loot overhaul across most vanilla structures - world exploration emphasis + reward scaling for structures

Create: The Factory Must Grow - Create-native industrial addon, bridges to Stellaris below via its own compat mod. Pinned to 1.1.1 (not latest 1.2.0) because tfmg-stellaris-compat 1.1.1 requires tfmg in range [1.1.0, 1.2.0) and hasn't been updated for 1.2.0 yet - found via the actual boot error, not Modrinth's dependency metadata
required by Stellaris - not listed in Modrinth's own dependency metadata for our version, found only via the actual boot error (ModLoadingException naming it directly)
space travel: rocket crafting across planets/moons, the Starforged Age gateway per instructions.md's resolved space-travel decision (Ad Astra ruled out, stalled pre-1.21.1 NeoForge)
bridges Create: TFMG's industrial machines to Stellaris' rocket/planet progression - the reason Stellaris was chosen over other space mods
general startup/memory/loading-time optimization, both sides but especially server boot time
reduces heap memory usage - server performance is a major concern per instructions.md
concurrent chunk management (parallel gen/loading) - server-only, a heavily-modded worldgen stack is exactly where chunk loading is the biggest server bottleneck
network stack optimization (packet encoding/compression) - server-only, matters most under multiple simultaneous connections
worldgen performance (noise generation), complements c2me's parallelism rather than duplicating it
required by Allthemodium below (its own ore-gen companion mod)

gear overhaul (Phase 10): supplies Allthemodium/Vibranium/Unobtainium/Alloy as the Induction Age-and-beyond Silent Gear material tiers, per the user's explicit call to lean on a late-game meta-material mod rather than inventing bespoke space-tier materials. Its own native tools/armor/weapons are removed via KubeJS - only the raw materials are used, everything crafts through Silent Gear instead. Requires GeckoLib (already installed, Phase 7)
required by Building Wands below

utility overhaul: building wand/gadget for quick construction. Requires Architectury API (already installed, Phase 4) + Cloth Config above. Ships 5 hardcoded tiers (stone/copper/iron/diamond/netherite, a closed Java enum - no material registry to plug our own tiers into past netherite) plus a 3-tier Magic Bag block-source item, each gated behind the matching ProgressiveStages tier via KubeJS item locks
required by Sophisticated Backpacks below

utility overhaul: general expandable-inventory backpacks (6 hardcoded tiers, leather/copper/iron/gold/diamond/netherite - same closed-tier-ladder situation as Building Wands) plus the separate 'Miner's Pouch' progression line - Void/Filter/Stack Upgrade items socketed into any backpack for voidable bulk per-item storage, tier-gated independently of the backpack's own slot-count progression
required by Create Aeronautics below - confirmed via the jar's own neoforge.mods.toml (versionRange [2.0.0,3.0.0)), not just Modrinth's dependency metadata

travel overhaul (revised): planes/airships, replacing Immersive Aircraft per user request. The bundled jar jarjar-embeds Offroad and Simulated - re-inspected the actual 1.3.0 jar and found it now DOES ship real recipes (data/aeronautics/recipe/*), reversing the earlier phase's 'zero recipe files' finding which was true of an older release. Gating is entirely implicit via recipe-ingredient swaps (KubeJS, see travel.js) reusing materials this pack already tier-locks, rather than explicit ProgressiveStages item locks: Propeller Bearing already needs create:brass_casing (naturally Brass Age); Gyroscopic Propeller Bearing/Smart Propeller patched to need create:sturdy_sheet (Precision Age); Levitite Blend already needs crushed end_stone, naturally Precision-Age-gated since this pack locks the End itself until then; Steam Vent patched to need allthemodium:allthemodium_ingot (Induction Age, this pack's existing top material) as the ceiling before the Tier 5 space gateway; Adjustable Burner patched to need create:brass_sheet so basic burner-driven flight launches alongside the Bearing at Brass Age
required by Waystones below

travel overhaul: teleportation, explicitly gated late per the user's request. All recipes use only vanilla materials (ender pearl, amethyst shard, emerald, obsidian, stone bricks) with nothing naturally tier-locked in this pack, so gating is entirely explicit ProgressiveStages item locks at induction_age - the automation ceiling just before the Tier 5 space gateway
required by Terralith below - cross-mod biome region placement API
required by Terralith below - not listed in Modrinth's own dependency metadata for our version, found only via the actual boot error ('Mod terralith requires lithostitched 1.7.7 or above')

world exploration overhaul: ~100 new overworld biomes plus several new immersive structures, built entirely from vanilla blocks - no new items, so no economy/gen_economy.py work needed. Directly satisfies instructions.md's 'the world must be varied in content and biomes'
world exploration overhaul: new themed roguelike structures spread across many biomes (desert/ice/jungle/mushroom/sky/swamp/savanna dungeons etc.) - structure variety
world exploration overhaul: small atmospheric lore structures with their own loot - structure variety, low risk/no hard deps

world exploration overhaul: overhauls abandoned mineshafts into biome-themed variants (dirt/sandy/mesa/snowy/ice/jungle/ocean) with more elaborate layouts. Requires yungs-api (already installed, Phase 8)
world exploration overhaul: overhauls strongholds with more varied rooms and loot. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls desert pyramids with expanded interiors and loot. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls jungle temples with expanded interiors and loot. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls Nether fortresses into larger, more varied layouts. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls swamp huts with expanded interiors and loot. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls ocean monuments with more varied rooms and loot. Requires yungs-api
world exploration overhaul: overhauls the central End island approach/exit structures. Requires yungs-api

TODO.md item 1: Create-native (rotational-force-powered machines) large excavatable ore vein deposits - a simpler/richer alternative to manually-placed vanilla ore scatter. Only hard dependency is Create itself (already installed); JEI/CC:Tweaked integrations are optional and not installed

TODO.md item 7: block-based chunk loading through Create itself - two loader tiers named andesite/brass (matching this pack's own tier ladder 1:1), must spin under real kinetic power and continuously draw Stress to keep loading (speedMultiplier/stressImpact per-tier config, verified by decompiling its config classes); STATIC/CONTRAPTION/TRAIN/STATION modes. Only hard dep is Create [6.0.0,) (installed 6.0.10); JEI optional. FTB Chunks' own menu-based force-loading is disabled in pack/config/ftbchunks-world.snbt (claims stay), per the recorded decision

TODO.md item 4 (scaffold only - jetpack->creative-flight mobility progression; wave-2 implements the mechanics): jetpacks (mod id create_sa). Verified via its own neoforge.mods.toml: requires minecraft [1.21.1] exact + neoforge [21.1.65,) (installed 21.1.235 satisfies); create itself is listed OPTIONAL despite the mod's name/description leaning entirely on Create's aesthetic. Ships 4 chestplate-style jetpacks natively (copper/andesite/brass/netherite, confirmed via its en_us.json lang file and item model/recipe file presence) - tier-locked 1:1 onto this pack's andesite/brass/precision/induction ages in the corresponding TOML files this same commit

TODO.md item 4 (scaffold only): addon for create-stuff-additions (mod id create_nj) adding a Netherite Exoskeleton plus its own separate Netherite Jetpack. Verified via its own neoforge.mods.toml: requires create AND create_sa both REQUIRED (Modrinth's own version-metadata API showed empty deps for 1.2 - misleading; jar ground truth is the real source), neoforge [21.1.176,) (installed 21.1.235 satisfies). GROUND-TRUTH FINDING for wave-2: create_nj:netherite_jetpack_chestplate is a second, independently-reciped netherite jetpack that duplicates create_sa's own native create_sa:netherite_jetpack_chestplate (a smithing upgrade from the brass jetpack) - both are locked at induction_age.toml this commit to prevent either bypassing the tier, but wave-2 should decide whether create_nj's copy belongs in TODO.md item 3's dedup pattern instead of staying a parallel item

TODO.md item 5 (scaffold only - discoverable/upgradeable Curios trinkets; wave-2 wires loot placement into scripts/gen_structure_loot.py's rarity tiers and builds the duplicate-combine mechanic): dozens of Curios-slot trinkets, mostly loot-found not craftable. TODO.md item 6 (scaffold only): required dependency of creeper-overhaul (confirmed via creeper-overhaul's own neoforge.mods.toml, [[dependencies.creeperoverhaul]] modId="resourcefulconfig" versionRange="[3.0.0,)"). Release client-optimization set (DECISIONS.md 'Client-optimization set - ADOPTED'): renderer replacement, FPS. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported confirms client-only. Create 6.0.10's bundled Flywheel 1.0.6 jar-in-jar declares sodium [0.6.0-beta.2,) as an optional CLIENT dep + ships real SodiumCompat classes - first-party Create support

Release client-optimization set: culls occluded entities/block-entities from rendering. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported. Config pre-populated at pack/config/entityculling.json: default blockEntityWhitelist already covers create:hose_pulley/create:rope_pulley and default tickCullingWhitelist already covers create:contraption family (verified via javap on dev.tr7zw.entityculling.versionless.Config's default-constructor bytecode); entityWhitelist extended with GeckoLib-animated entity ids from Ars Nouveau familiars/summons, Born in Chaos, and Naturalist (grounded via javap scan of each mod's renderer classes for software/bernie/geckolib references, cross-matched against each mod's en_us.json entity.<ns>.* keys - not guessed)

Release client-optimization set: batches/optimizes item-entity and GUI rendering. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported. Pinned to 1.6.11 (not just newest) to get past a narrow 1.6.10 Create-Aeronautics-Staff-of-Physics inventory-display bug per DECISIONS.md's watch-list

Release client-optimization set: additional occlusion-culling coverage beyond EntityCulling. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported. KNOWN RISK flagged in DECISIONS.md: its own neoforge.mods.toml declares minecraft dependency versionRange=[1.21,1.21.1) which textually EXCLUDES exact 1.21.1 (exclusive upper bound) - verified by javap/jar-extracting its toml. JEI's toml has the identical [1.21,1.21.1) pattern and is known-working on 1.21.1 everywhere, suggesting NeoForge may not enforce this range as strictly as it reads, but MoreCulling cannot be boot-tested on the dedicated server (client-only) - verdict deferred to the L2 HeadlessMC client-smoke stage; DROP from manifest+lockfile (not pin/patch) if it fails to load there

Release client-optimization set: throttles rendering/tick rate when the client window is unfocused/minimized. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported. Optional dependency on cloth-config (already installed, phase 11) for its config screen

Release client-optimization set: merges XP orbs to cut entity-count lag, matching the c2me/krypton convention of side markers driven by the jar's actual client/server support. Modrinth client_side=optional/server_side=optional confirms side:both is correct (it does real work server-side merging XP orbs, not just a client renderer)

Release client QoL (user decision, DECISIONS.md 'Client QoL mods'): recipe viewer, near-mandatory given this pack's recipe-gating identity. Modrinth client_side=optional/server_side=optional; jar's own neoforge.mods.toml has no side=CLIENT restriction on its deps - ships a real server-side recipe-sync component, side:both is correct

Release client QoL (user decision): exploration minimap for the ~100-biome/structure-hunt world overhaul. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=optional - the jar supports an optional server-side presence (server-side map data/caves rendering assist), so side:both per the standing rule ('side:both acceptable where the jar supports server presence'). Jar-in-jar embeds its own xaerolib-neoforge-1.21.1-1.5.0.jar hard dependency (verified via jar contents, META-INF/jarjar/) - NeoForge extracts jar-in-jar deps automatically, no separate manifest entry needed

Release client QoL (user decision): companion fullscreen map to Xaero's Minimap above. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=optional -> side:both, same reasoning as xaeros-minimap. Its own neoforge.mods.toml declares a REQUIRED dependency on modId=xaerolib (not just optional cross-mod integration) - satisfied by the same jar-in-jar embed, verified present in this jar too

Release client QoL (user decision, pulled forward from the deferred item 9 food stack): hunger/saturation/exhaustion HUD overlay. Modrinth client_side=optional/server_side=optional - full accuracy needs a matching server-side install for real exhaustion values (client-only falls back to estimation), so side:both is correct over client-only

TODO.md item 10 (tick accelerator): companion fix addon (modId tiabfix, jar tiab-entity-fix-1.0.3.jar - note the jar's own internal toml version field reads '1.0.0', a cosmetic mismatch against the Modrinth version_number/filename, both pinned exactly per DECISIONS.md). Required for crop/animal acceleration to work at all in modern TiaB versions (DECISIONS.md). TODO.md item 9: required lib for create-central-kitchen below (its own neoforge.mods.toml declares create_dragons_plus [1.11.1,) REQUIRED). Verified via its own toml: only hard dep besides loader/MC is create [6.0.10,) (already installed since phase 0).

TODO.md item 9: Farmer's Delight cutting-board -> Create Saw/Deployer automation bridge (chosen over Slice & Dice per TODO's own reasoning - reuses base Create blocks, no Kotlin-for-Forge dep). Verified via its own toml: create [6.0.10,) + create_dragons_plus [1.11.1,) both REQUIRED, farmersdelight optional (ships conditional integration for 5 different -delight mods, all of which this pack has except miners/dungeons-delight/brewinandchewin). GROUND-TRUTHED (not assumed) Saw-conversion mechanism: only farmersdelight:cutting recipes whose tool Ingredient accepts a plain Iron Knife get exposed on the Saw - 47/106 FD + 78/106 extradelight + 9/10 ends-delight recipes qualify directly; the rest are Deployer-automatable by holding the matching axe/hoe/pickaxe/shovel/shears/spoon (all genuine members of Create's own handheld_in_deployer_use tool tag). The only 2 food-producing exceptions (End's Delight's dragon-egg cracking needing a literal Nether Star, ExtraDelight's 8 grater recipes needing a literal untagged Grater item) are patched via pack/kubejs/server_scripts/food_cck_gap_patch.js (adds a knife-tool alternate recipe for each, inheriting the same proven Saw path) rather than left as a silent automation gap.

TODO.md item 9: required lib for spice-of-life-onion below (its own toml requires creativecore [2.13.32,) - our 2.13.41 comfortably satisfies) and hosts SoL-Onion's own config framework (CreativeConfig, a Gson-JSON system separate from NeoForge's TOML ModConfigSpec - config/solonion.json, see solonion_config_guidance.md in this handoff dir). Verified via its own toml: no other hard deps beyond loader/MC.

TODO.md item 9: the diet-variety mechanic itself (Spice-of-Life-Carrot-style permanent bonus hearts at distinct-foods-eaten milestones, reward-only per user decision - no detriments/food groups). Verified via its own toml: creativecore [2.13.32,) REQUIRED (satisfied), neoforge [21.1,), minecraft [1.21.1,1.22). Detection is registry-wide (any item with vanilla FoodProperties counts, from any mod) - Create Stuff & Additions/Naturalist foods count automatically, no extra KubeJS wiring needed. REQUIRES a config override away from stock defaults to match the user's 'permanent, reward-only' decision - see solonion_config_guidance.md (resetOnDeath/trackedFoodDiversityDecay both default to true upstream; detriments already defaults to empty).
